description: 3 web folds on apache lack wicket/toolchain coverage

W-01.label: target_reachable_and_responsive
W-01.description: Target system or service responds to network probes; collection or exploitation is feasible.
W-01.evidence_hint: TCP connect or ICMP probe confirms reachability; confirm via port scan or HTTP HEAD request

W-02.label: target_service_exposed
W-02.description: Target service is listening on a reachable port and accepting connections.
W-02.evidence_hint: Service banner grab or HTTP 200/401 response confirms service is listening

W-03.label: apache_high_vuln_present
W-03.description: ScriptAlias directory in NCSA and Apache httpd allowed attackers to read CGI programs. (CVE-1999-0236, CVSS 7.5)
W-03.evidence_hint: Network-accessible service probe; confirm reachability via TCP connect or banner grab

W-04.label: cve_2009_1955_denial_of_service_possible
W-04.description: The expat XML parser in the apr_xml_* interface in xml/apr_xml.c in Apache APR-util before 1.3.7, as used in the mod_dav and mod_dav_svn modules in the Apache HTTP Server, allows remote attackers to c (CVE-2009-1955, CVSS 7.5)
W-04.evidence_hint: Network-accessible service probe; confirm reachability via TCP connect or banner grab

W-05.label: cve_2009_2699_denial_of_service_possible
W-05.description: The Solaris pollset feature in the Event Port backend in poll/unix/port.c in the Apache Portable Runtime (APR) library before 1.3.9, as used in the Apache HTTP Server before 2.2.14 and other products, (CVE-2009-2699, CVSS 7.5)
W-05.evidence_hint: Network-accessible service probe; confirm reachability via TCP connect or banner grab

web_network_exploit_v1.description: Remote exploitation via network-accessible vulnerability in web environment.

web_local_exploit_v1.description: Local exploitation of web environment requiring initial access.

web_full_chain_v1.description: Full exploitation chain: remote access through local privilege escalation in web.
